specific_location: Barr Lake State Park banding station
description: The bluish-gray crown and face contrasted boldly with the olive nape and back. The wing bars were whitish, and the lores and eye rings, which were broken in front of the eyes, were thick and white. The throat and underparts were white with yellowish olive flanks. With the exception of the tertails, which were edged in white, the edging of the secondaries was green. The edging of the outer rectrices was distinct and white. This BHVI was aged as Hatching Year due to the pale, grayish white upper mandible lining and incomplete pneumaticization of its skull.Behaviors: Normal behaviors of the Blue-headed Vireo were not observed during its capture and banding.Call: not applicablePlumage: blue crown olive upperpts white underpts
photographer: Photographer: Steven Brown, Steven Brown, Steven Brown, Steven Brown
